articles.description: Blockchain trackers put the cryptocurrency heist in the hundreds of millions of dollars and is already on track to be the largest crypto theft in 2026 so far.

articles.title: Anthropic took down thousands of GitHub repos trying to yank its leaked source code — a move the company says was an accident
articles.description: Anthropic executives said it was an accident and retracted the bulk of the takedown notices.

articles.title: The reputation of troubled YC startup Delve has gotten even worse
articles.description: Delve faces new allegations that it violated the open source license of its customer, Sim.ai, by taking the customers's tool and passing it off as its own.

articles.title: ‘System failure’ paralyzes Baidu robotaxis in China
articles.description: Passengers in Baidu's robotaxis were trapped for up to two hours.

articles.title: A new dating app, Sonder, has a deliberately annoying sign-up process (and it’s working)
articles.description: Sonder profiles are completely unstructured, encouraging users to build something that looks like a mood board or a digital collage. Think MySpace rather than LinkedIn.

articles.title: Startup funding shatters all records in Q1
articles.description: The record quarterly fundraise largely fueled four mega-deals into  OpenAI, Anthropic, xAI, and Waymo. But it also indicates a generally hot market.

articles.title: Apple releases security fix for older iPhones and iPads to protect against DarkSword attacks
articles.description: The security update protects a raft of older iPhones and iPads from attacks linked to leaked hacking tools called DarkSword.

articles.title: Meta’s natural gas binge could power South Dakota
articles.description: Meta's upcoming Hyperion AI data center will be powered by 10 new natural gas plants.

articles.title: Cameo partners with TikTok to boost popularity
articles.description: Cameo launched a new TikTok integration that allows U.S. creators to offer personalized videos through the app.

articles.title: WhatsApp notifies hundreds of users who installed a fake app made by government spyware maker
articles.description: The Meta-owned company said it identified around 200 users who were tricked into installing a fake version of WhatsApp that was actually Italian-made spyware.

articles.title: Cognichip wants AI to design the chips that power AI, and just raised $60M to try
articles.description: The firm says it can reduce the cost of chip development by more than 75% and cut the timeline by more than half.

articles.title: SpaceX files confidentially for IPO in mega listing potentially valued at $1.75 trillion, report says
articles.description: The company has also lined up an unusually large number of 21 banks to manage the mega IPO, internally codenamed “Project Apex."

articles.title: Hasbro says it was hacked, and may take ‘several weeks’ to recover
articles.description: The American toy-making giant noted that it was continuing to "implement measures to secure its business operations," suggesting that the hackers may still be in the company's systems.

articles.title: Less than a month: StrictlyVC San Francisco brings leaders from TDK Ventures, Replit, and more together
articles.description: StrictlyVC San Francisco brings leaders from TDK Ventures, Replit, and more together on April 30. Space is limited. Register here for your pass.

articles.title: Lucid Motors recalls over 4,000 Gravity SUVs citing improperly welded seat belts
articles.description: The company struggled with quality issues when Gravity production started last year, and the new recall shows it's not out of the woods yet.

articles.title: Nothing’s AI devices plan reportedly contains smart glasses and earbuds
articles.description: The glasses will reportedly feature cameras, microphones, and speakers, and will connect to a smartphone and the cloud to process AI queries.

articles.title: Mercor says it was hit by cyberattack tied to compromise of open source LiteLLM project
articles.description: The AI recruiting startup confirmed a security incident after an extortion hacking crew took credit for stealing data from the company's systems.

articles.title: Toyota’s Woven Capital appoints new CIO and COO in push for finding the ‘future of mobility’
articles.description: Woven Capital is the growth-stage venture capital arm of Toyota, focused on backing founders building in space, cybersecurity, and autonomous driving.

digest:
## 🔬 Major Technical Developments

### EmDash – A spiritual successor to WordPress that solves plugin security
URL: https://blog.cloudflare.com/emdash-wordpress/
Cloudflare builds WordPress replacement with security-first architecture
WordPress powers 40%+ of the web but its plugin ecosystem is a persistent attack vector. Cloudflare entering CMS territory signals they see enough enterprise pain to justify building rather than patching. Watch for hosting provider responses.

### A new C++ back end for ocamlc
URL: https://github.com/ocaml/ocaml/pull/14701
OCaml compiler adds C++ codegen, expanding interop surface
Niche but meaningful for Jane Street and other OCaml-heavy shops. C++ backend improves FFI ergonomics and potentially unlocks performance paths. Signals OCaml community investment in staying relevant for systems work.

### Cognichip wants AI to design the chips that power AI, and just raised $60M to try
URL: https://techcrunch.com/2026/04/01/cognichip-wants-ai-to-design-the-chips-that-power-ai-and-just-raised-60m-to-try/
AI-driven EDA startup claims 75% cost reduction in chip development
Bold claims on timeline and cost compression in semiconductor design. If validated, this threatens Synopsys/Cadence duopoly. At $60M, still early—watch for design tape-out announcements and foundry partnerships.

## 💼 Business & Market Signals

### SpaceX files confidentially for IPO in mega listing potentially valued at $1.75 trillion
URL: https://techcrunch.com/2026/04/01/spacex-files-confidentially-for-ipo-in-mega-listing-potentially-valued-at-1-75-trillion-report-says/
21 banks lined up for what would be the largest tech IPO ever
$1.75T would make SpaceX worth more than Alphabet. The 21-bank syndicate is unprecedented and suggests massive retail allocation strategy. Starlink revenue trajectory and government contract visibility will be the story in the S-1.

### Startup funding shatters all records in Q1
URL: https://techcrunch.com/2026/04/01/startup-funding-shatters-all-records-in-q1/
Mega-rounds into OpenAI, Anthropic, xAI, and Waymo drive historic quarter
Concentration risk is the headline beneath the headline—four companies captured the bulk of deployment. Signals bifurcation: frontier AI and autonomy attract unlimited capital, everything else fights for scraps.

### Meta's natural gas binge could power South Dakota
URL: https://techcrunch.com/2026/04/01/metas-natural-gas-binge-could-power-south-dakota/
Hyperion data center will require 10 new gas plants
AI infrastructure build-out hitting real energy constraints. Meta choosing gas over renewables is a pragmatic admission that current green supply can't meet AI power demands. Watch for utility company capex revisions and NatGas infrastructure plays.

### De-fi platform Drift suspends deposits and withdrawals after millions in crypto stolen in hack
URL: https://techcrunch.com/2026/04/01/de-fi-platform-drift-suspends-deposits-and-withdrawals-after-millions-in-crypto-stolen-in-hack/
Hundreds of millions stolen in what may be 2026's largest crypto theft
DeFi security remains fundamentally unsolved. Combined with the Mercor/LiteLLM supply chain compromise, we're seeing coordinated sophistication in attacks. Insurance and audit providers are the beneficiaries here.

## 📋 Case Studies & Deep Dives

### Mercor says it was hit by cyberattack tied to compromise of open source LiteLLM project
URL: https://techcrunch.com/2026/03/31/mercor-says-it-was-hit-by-cyberattack-tied-to-compromise-of-open-source-litellm-project/
Supply chain attack through AI tooling dependency hits YC-backed startup
This is the Log4j pattern repeating in AI infrastructure. LiteLLM is widely used for LLM routing—one compromised dependency, many downstream victims. Enterprise AI adoption will require serious supply chain auditing.

### The reputation of troubled YC startup Delve has gotten even worse
URL: https://techcrunch.com/2026/04/01/the-reputation-of-troubled-yc-startup-delve-has-gotten-even-worse/
Open source license violations add to Delve's mounting credibility problems
Allegations of passing off customer's OSS tool as proprietary product. Pattern of governance failures at YC companies this cycle worth monitoring—diligence on IP provenance becoming non-negotiable.

### 'System failure' paralyzes Baidu robotaxis in China
URL: https://techcrunch.com/2026/04/01/system-failure-paralyzes-baidu-robotaxis-in-china/
Passengers trapped for two hours in fleet-wide autonomous vehicle outage
Centralized fleet management creates single points of failure at scale. Baidu's Apollo program is China's Waymo bet—this incident will trigger regulatory scrutiny and potentially slow deployment permits.

## ⚡ Signal vs. Noise

The capital concentration into frontier AI (four companies dominating Q1 fundraising) combined with real-world infrastructure constraints (Meta building 10 gas plants) suggests we're past the experimentation phase and into industrial buildout. Security is emerging as the binding constraint—supply chain attacks on AI tooling (LiteLLM), DeFi exploits, and fleet-wide autonomous vehicle failures all point to systems complexity outpacing defensive capabilities. The SpaceX IPO at potentially $1.75T would reset expectations for what a technology company can be worth, but the more interesting signal is the 21-bank syndicate: everyone wants exposure, nobody wants to miss allocation.
